summary: This guest post by Maxwell Feiner, a high school student, introduces three free online math tools he uses regularly. Brilliant.org provides challenging problems that require deeper thinking beyond standard formulas. Desmos offers an interactive graphing calculator with powerful features like adjustable variables and animations. WolframAlpha serves as a computational engine for graphing and solving various mathematical queries. The post includes practical examples and tutorials for each tool.
key_points: Brilliant.org functions as a social platform for challenging math problems that require insight beyond standard classroom methods
Desmos allows users to create interactive graphs with adjustable variables, sliders, animations, and movable points
WolframAlpha can handle 1-D, 2-D, and 3-D graphing plus a wide range of computational queries
All three tools are free to use and accessible to students
money_quotes: Think of it as a social site for math.
I like the problems a lot because they require insight beyond what is taught in school classes.
They normally cannot be solved using one formula or pre-learned method, but instead require deeper thought and a combination of different concepts in order to be solved.
It is simple to use, but at the same time very powerful.
This is just barely scratching the surface of what can be done with the powerful tool.
key_insight: Free online tools like Brilliant, Desmos, and WolframAlpha can significantly enhance math learning by providing challenging problems and powerful computational aids that go beyond traditional classroom resources.
